title: Drejerella guttata
taxonomy: Drejerella guttata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales and is a member of the family Gesneriaceae. This classification places Drejerella guttata within the group of flowering plants commonly known as the "bells" due to their bell-shaped flowers. The plant is native to Asia and is a type of epiphytic species, which means it grows on other plants or objects without deriving nutrients from them. Drejerella guttata is a relatively rare species and is valued for its unique and attractive flowers.
